seoTitle: Mount Burrell vs Mount Collins — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Burrell and Mount Collins. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Burrell vs Mount Collins: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Burrell and Mount Collins.
heading: Mount Burrell vs Mount Collins — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Burrell, New South Wales and Mount Collins, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Burrell has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Mount Collins (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Burrell or Mount Collins safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Burrell has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Mount Collins scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Burrell scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Collins scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Burrell or Mount Collins?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Burrell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
